title.rendered: Wrongful Death Attorneys in  Fresno & Fresno CA

content.rendered:
}">

Dealing with the loss of a loved one can be devastating. Worst of all, some people feel helpless to do anything about it. At Chuckstern Murphy Law Firm, we help victims’ families get the compensation they deserve for the wrongful death of a loved one.

We have a team of wrongful death attorneys in Fresno and Fresno County; including Fresno, Fresno, Thousand Oaks, Camarillo, Burbank, and Newbury Park. We stand up for the rights of victims who have lost the life of a loved one due to negligence or criminal intent from another party.

}">

How to File a Wrongful Death Suit

Our team of attorneys at the Chuckstern Murphy Law Firm will help you file a wrongful death suit in a civil court to sue the responsible party. Generally, any surviving family member can bring a wrongful death suit, including:

  • Spouse
  • Children
  • Immediate family members
  • Any dependents

Compensation may be awarded for emotional damages, loss of love and support, funeral costs, medical bills, and money lost as a result of the death of a loved one. Don’t wait to file a claim; get compensation and justice now!

Additionally, we can handle the distribution of an estate or trust after a wrongful death.

Determining Fault for a Wrongful Death Suit

Our team of trained attorneys will investigate wrongful death suits on your behalf to determine whether you have a case and who is at fault. While most wrongful death suits don’t generally make it to court, our attorneys have been successful in the past at getting claims to court and getting compensation for our clients.

Because wrongful death suits are handled in front of a jury in civil court, the burden of proof is much lower than proving criminal intent. Generally, we will have to establish negligence, causation, damages, and breach of duty on the part of the defendant.

Even in motor accidents, it can be argued that the motorist had a duty to abide by traffic laws that ensure the safety of other motorists. From there, we will work to determine proper compensation for the damages that occurred on the part of the plaintiff (you).

Statute of Limitation for a Wrongful Death Suit in California

The time following the death of a loved one can be confusing and stressful. That’s why California allows families up to two years to file a wrongful death suit for the accidental death of a loved one. Anyone from stepchildren to dependents of the deceased can seek compensation for a wrongful death suit.

Come to an experienced team of lawyers who will help your family through this trying time and give you some peace of mind.

Filing a Wrongful Death Suit in Fresno

Trust Chuckstern Murphy Law Firm to handle your case with the care and respect you need. We will work tirelessly to help your family receive the compensation you deserve and help recover any costs associated with a wrongful death suit. Trust an experienced team of wrongful death lawyers in Fresno and Fresno County to handle your case with decency and respect.

Find out how we can help you by calling our Fresno office at 415-217-9456 or our Fresno office at 415-217-9456. Or, sign up for a free consultation online or contact us online at our Fresno or Fresno offices.
